Job description

Computer Science Intern – Revmo AI (Paid)

 

About Revmo AI

Revmo AI is a Phoenix-based SaaS start-up building industry-leading voice-AI agents for high-volume, service-oriented businesses (restaurants, automotive service, hospitality, and more). Our platform answers and routes millions of customer calls and texts, integrates in real time with POS, CRM, and payment systems, and surfaces rich analytics that drive revenue and guest satisfaction.

You’ll join a small, fast-moving engineering team shipping production code used daily by brands like Donatos Pizza and Jiffy Lube.


Internship Snapshot

Job type

Paid internship (Part-time during semesters ⬩ Full-time option in summer)

Location

Revmo HQ (200 E Van Buren, Phoenix AZ – next to ASU downtown campus)

Duration

12–16 weeks, with potential to extend or convert to full-time

Compensation

$22–$30 per hour, based on experience

Start

Rolling start dates beginning May 2026 (flexible around class schedules)

Hours

15–20 hrs/week during the semester or 40 hrs/week in summer

Candidate Notes

Graduate students (M.S. or 4+1 students in their final year) are strongly encouraged to apply.
This role is designed as an internship-to-full-time pathway for high-performing interns who are graduating in the next 6–12 months.
At this time, Revmo cannot sponsor work visas for this internship or for full-time employment post-graduation. Candidates must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a long-term basis without the need for current or future employer sponsorship.
We are especially interested in candidates who have built OpenClaw-style agent systems: self-hosted or local-first assistants, long-lived control planes or gateways, multi-channel agent workflows, custom skills/tools, agent routing, scheduled automations, and safe tool usage. Direct OpenClaw experience is not required, but adjacent experience is a strong plus. 

 

What You’ll Do

Work side-by-side with senior engineers to design, code, test, and deploy Python-based microservices that power our conversational AI platform.
Build and maintain agent tools, skills, and integrations that allow Revmo’s voice and messaging agents to take action inside real business workflows.
Integrate third-party APIs (Twilio, POS vendors, CRM systems, payment processors, calendars, internal APIs) into our agentic integration layer for real-time ordering, scheduling, payment, and follow-up actions.
Prototype and harden LLM-based agent capabilities such as tool calling, retrieval, memory, routing, evaluation, and background automations.
Help design guardrails around tool permissions, trust boundaries, observability, and prompt-injection resistance so agent workflows are reliable in production.
Participate in code reviews, agile ceremonies, and weekly Lunch-and-Learns on AI/ML, software craftsmanship, and start-up life.
Ship meaningful work—past interns have pushed production features within their first month. 

Learning Objectives

Apply CS fundamentals (data structures, algorithms, databases) to production-scale, event-driven agent systems.
Gain hands-on experience with cloud-native architecture (AWS, Docker, Kubernetes), CI/CD, and real-time integrations.
Deepen understanding of speech recognition, NLU, LLM tool use, retrieval, evaluation, and agent security.
Strengthen professional skills—communication, agile teamwork, and technical ownership—while building software that directly impacts customer conversations.

Required Qualifications

Current ASU graduate student (M.S. or 4+1) in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Computer Systems Engineering, Information Technology, or a closely related field.
Expected graduation within the next 6–12 months.
Strong grasp of Python and at least one additional modern programming language (TypeScript/Node.js, Java, or Go).
Coursework or project experience in data structures/algorithms, web services (REST, gRPC, or WebSockets), and backend development.
Hands-on experience building at least one AI/LLM-enabled application, automation, or agent workflow.
Familiarity with Git/GitHub workflows and shipping code in a team or project environment.
Ability to work a consistent schedule—15+ hrs/week during the semester and up to 40 hrs/week in summer.
Current authorization to work in the United States that does not require employer sponsorship now or in the future.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience with OpenClaw or similar agent frameworks/platforms, especially work involving custom skills, plugins, tools, routing, sessions, or self-hosted agent runtimes.  ￼
Experience integrating external systems such as Twilio, Stripe, Slack, Discord, Telegram, calendars, CRMs, POS vendors, or other SaaS APIs.
Exposure to retrieval, vector databases, prompt engineering, agent evaluation, or model serving.
Comfort with Docker, Redis, PostgreSQL, queues, WebSockets, AWS Lambda/SQS/S3, or other event-driven infrastructure.
Experience reasoning about prompt injection, scoped credentials, sandboxing, allowlists, and tool safety in agentic systems.  
Passion for voice tech, conversational AI, and agent systems that solve real operational problems for busy service businesses.

Why Revmo AI

Impact from Day 1 – your code will touch live calls within weeks, not months.
Mentorship – direct access to founders, principal engineers, and recent ASU alum Alex Burton, whose intern → engineer journey we hope to replicate for you.
Cutting-edge stack – LLMs, vector DBs, streaming analytics, real-time integrations, and agent tooling—no legacy monoliths.
Real agent systems, not toy demos – you’ll work on production workflows where AI agents talk to customers and take actions in live business systems.
Start-up energy, real stability – VC-backed, revenue-generating, growing fast.
Path to full-time – high-performing interns are strongly considered for return offers.

How to Apply

Submit the following through Handshake:

Resume (PDF)
Brief cover note explaining why voice-AI agent systems or OpenClaw-style tooling excites you. Please also include your expected graduation date and confirmation of U.S. work authorization without sponsorship.
Link to GitHub, portfolio, or a class project you are proud of, ideally demonstrating work with Python, APIs, agent tools, or LLMs.

Note: Applications that are missing any of the items above (1–3) or contain an incomplete cover note will not be considered for review.

 

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis; early applicants receive priority for summer and fall 2026 start dates.

 

Revmo AI is an equal-opportunity employer and embraces diversity of thought, background, and experience. We look forward to building the future of conversational AI with the next generation of Sun Devil engineers.
